meaning: The container for a set of axioms, declarations, and annotations under a single document/IRI — the named unit holding all class/property/individual statements plus metadata (version, imports).
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Header of an SBVR vocabulary: "Vocabulary: Pizza Ontology (version 2.0)." Names the whole rule/term set rather than producing a per-fact sentence.

meaning: Declares the current ontology depends on and incorporates all axioms of another ontology by IRI; imported axioms are treated as part of the importing ontology for reasoning. (Distinct from owl:versionIRI; import is by ontology-IRI document retrieval.)
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): This vocabulary includes the FOAF Vocabulary. Every imported term and rule becomes available here.

meaning: A named category whose instances are individuals sharing some property; denotes a set of individuals — introduces a general concept (a type) into the vocabulary.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Declares a general noun concept: "Pizza is a general concept." (An owl:Class becomes an object type / general concept.)

meaning: Asserts every instance of the subclass is necessarily also an instance of the superclass — a one-directional "is-a-kind-of" inclusion; does not imply the reverse.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Each Margherita is a Pizza.

meaning: Asserts two class expressions have exactly the same instances in every interpretation — mutual subclassing; membership in one is necessary and sufficient for the other.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Definitional rule: "A Vegetarian Pizza is by definition a Pizza that has only vegetarian toppings."

meaning: Asserts two class expressions (not necessarily named atomic classes) share no individuals: nothing can be an instance of both at once — a pairwise mutual-exclusion statement between the two operands of a single axiom.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): No Pizza is a Pizza Base.

meaning: A single axiom stating all classes in a list (given via owl:members, which may contain arbitrary class expressions) are pairwise mutually exclusive — no individual belongs to more than one. Compactly expresses disjointness across many at once.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): No Margherita is an American, no Margherita is a Soho, and no American is a Soho.

meaning: A relationship linking one individual to another individual; its values are objects (individuals), not literals — a binary relation between members of classes.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Fact type linking two things: "Each Pizza has some Topping."

meaning: A relationship linking an individual to a data value (a literal — string, number, date); values come from a datatype rather than being individuals.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Attribute-style fact type: "Each Pizza has at most one price (a decimal)."

meaning: A property attaching human-readable or bookkeeping metadata (labels, comments, provenance) to entities. Annotations carry no logical/inferential consequences for the OWL semantics (they do not affect entailment), though tools still read and present them.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Non-normative note: rdfs:label "Margherita" supplies the human term; rdfs:comment "Named after Queen Margherita" is a Note attached to the concept, not a rule.

meaning: States any individual that is the subject of the property must belong to the named class — constrains what can have the relationship and lets a reasoner infer the subject's type.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Anything that has a topping is a Pizza.

meaning: States any value that is the object of the property must belong to the named class or datatype — constrains what the relationship can point to and lets a reasoner infer the value's type.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Anything that is a topping of something is a Topping.

meaning: Asserts that whenever the subproperty relates two things, the superproperty relates them too — a one-directional inclusion between relations.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): That a thing has a spicy topping implies that the thing has a topping.

meaning: States two properties are reverses: if the first relates A to B, the second relates B to A, and vice versa — the two directions kept synchronized.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): That a Pizza has a Topping means that the Topping is a topping of the Pizza.

meaning: Asserts each individual is linked to at most one value through this property; two such values must be the same individual (or the data values equal). (OWL 2 DL caveat: functional/inverse-functional object properties may not be composite or transitive.)
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Each Pizza has at most one base.

meaning: Asserts each value is linked from at most one individual — the value uniquely identifies its subject (acts like a key); two individuals sharing the value must be the same.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Each social security number is the social security number of at most one Person.

meaning: Asserts the relation chains: if A relates to B and B to C, then A relates to C — carries through intermediate steps automatically.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): If a region is part of a second region and that second is part of a third, then the first is part of the third.

meaning: Asserts the relation holds both ways: if A relates to B, then B relates to A — the property is its own inverse.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): If a Person is married to a second Person, then the second is married to the first.

meaning: Asserts the relation can never hold both ways: if A relates to B, then B cannot relate to A — the strict opposite of symmetric.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): If a Person is a parent of a second Person, then the second is not a parent of the first.

meaning: Asserts every individual is related to itself by this property — holds between a thing and itself over the whole domain.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Each Thing knows itself.

meaning: Asserts no individual is ever related to itself by this property — can never hold between a thing and itself.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): No Person is a parent of himself or herself.

meaning: The class of individuals having at least one value of a property in a specified class — requires the existence of some related thing of the right kind (not that all are).
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Each Margherita has at least one topping that is a Mozzarella Topping.

meaning: The class of individuals all of whose values for a property fall in a specified class — constrains every related value, but does not require any exist.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Each Vegetarian Pizza has only toppings that are Vegetarian Toppings.

meaning: (Unqualified) the class of individuals having at least N distinct values for a property, regardless of those values' type — a lower bound on how many times the relationship must hold.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Each Pizza has at least two toppings.

meaning: (Unqualified, no type filter) the class of individuals having at most N distinct values for a property — an upper bound.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Each Pizza has at most four toppings.

meaning: (Unqualified, no type filter) the class of individuals having exactly N distinct values for a property — fixes the count precisely (both min and max).
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Each Pizza has exactly one base.

meaning: A cardinality restriction also constraining the type of counted values: individuals having exactly N values of a property that belong to a class (via owl:onClass) or datatype. Qualified min/max variants exist.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Each Quattro Formaggi has exactly four toppings that are Cheese Toppings.

meaning: The class of individuals related by a property to one specific named individual or data value — the target is a particular value, not a class.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Each Italian Pizza has the country of origin Italy.

meaning: Defines a class by exhaustively listing its members — the class consists of exactly the named individuals and nothing else (a closed enumeration).
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): A Spice Level is one of: Mild, Medium, Hot.

meaning: A class of individuals belonging to all listed class expressions simultaneously — logical conjunction (AND) over classes.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): A Vegetarian Pizza is a Pizza that has only vegetarian toppings.

meaning: A class of individuals belonging to at least one of the listed class expressions — logical disjunction (OR).
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): A Cheesy Topping is a Mozzarella Topping or a Parmesan Topping or a Gorgonzola Topping.

meaning: A class of all individuals NOT members of the given class — logical negation relative to the universe of all things.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): A Non-Vegetarian Pizza is a thing that is not a Vegetarian Pizza.

meaning: A specific, named member of the domain — a concrete thing (not a class or property) identified by an IRI; an instance that can belong to classes and participate in property relationships.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Individual concept / instance: "Italy is a Country."

meaning: Asserts two individual names refer to the very same individual — anything true of one is true of the other.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): The individual Italia is the same individual as Italy.

meaning: Asserts two individual names refer to distinct individuals — guaranteed not the same thing; blocks a reasoner from merging them.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): The individual Italy is a different individual from France.

meaning: The universal (top) class containing every individual in the domain; every class is implicitly a subclass of it and every individual an instance.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Everything is a Thing. Used as the unrestricted concept.

meaning: The empty (bottom) class containing no individuals; every class implicitly has it as a subclass — a class inferred equivalent to it is unsatisfiable.
sbvrReading (TSV; '#' line = comment): Nothing is a member of Nothing. An inferred "Each Vegan Meat Pizza is Nothing" means that concept is contradictory.
